$comment: Normative source tree: https://github.com/intentproof/intentproof-spec/tree/main/schema — $id is a logical URI; see README.
title: IntentProof ExecutionEvent v1

id.description: Stable unique identifier for this execution record.

intent.description: Natural-language description of what the user or caller was trying to achieve (often a full sentence or question). No fixed grammar; SHOULD stay human-readable in logs and UIs.

action.description: Stable identifier for the concrete operation. Many systems use hierarchical dotted names (e.g. vendor.resource.operation); REST paths, RPC names, or other conventions are allowed. The schema does not constrain the format.

inputs.description: Captured call inputs. Values MUST be JSON-serializable (see semantics/serialization_rules.md).

output.description: Captured return value when status is ok, or optional captured value when captureError allows output on failure.

error.description: Structured error when status is error.

startedAt.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 instant when execution started.

completedAt.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 instant when execution completed.

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration in milliseconds between startedAt and completedAt.

correlationId.description: Optional cross-cutting identifier for distributed tracing. MUST be trimmed and non-empty when present.

attributes.description: Flat primitive key/value metadata attached to the event.

name.description: Exception or error type name (e.g. Error, TypeError).

message.description: Human-readable error message (may be empty string if the runtime provides none).

stack.description: Optional stringified stack trace; null when stacks are suppressed.

cause.description: Optional chained cause; MUST conform to ExecutionError when present.
